description_long: This is a well-used official Major League Baseball, featuring a white leather exterior with prominent red stitching running in a double-curve pattern around its circumference. The surface shows visible signs of wear, including scuff marks, dirt, and discoloration, indicating it has been used in play. On one side, the iconic Major League Baseball logo is printed in black, while another side displays the 'World Series' emblem, also in black. There are two visible autographs in dark ink, one of which is partially visible and the other more prominent, adding a collectible aspect to the ball. The stitching is tightly sewn and stands out against the worn leather, and the ball's overall shape remains perfectly spherical. This baseball would be commonly found in urban parks, sports fields, or as a collectible in a garage or home.
description: A worn official Major League Baseball with red stitching, visible league logos, and autographs on a scuffed white leather surface.
description_view_0: This view shows the Major League Baseball logo in black, with red stitching and visible surface wear.
description_view_1: This side highlights two autographs in dark ink, with pronounced scuff marks and the red stitching curving around the ball.
description_view_2: The 'World Series' emblem is clearly visible in black, surrounded by worn white leather and red stitching.
description_view_3: This angle shows mostly the red stitching and a portion of an autograph, with less visible branding but clear surface wear.
attribute_stitch_pattern: Two curved seams with tightly spaced, raised red stitches running in a double-curve pattern around the ball.
attribute_brand_logo: Major League Baseball logo in black, located on one panel; design is a silhouette of a batter with a ball, clear and legible.
attribute_league_markings: 'World Series' emblem in black on one panel, featuring a trophy graphic and text; both logos are clear and distinct.
attribute_visible_wear_or_scuff_marks: Multiple scuff marks, dirt, and discoloration are visible across all panels, especially near the seams and around the autographs; the surface appears well-worn and used.
